mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW X5 xDrive45e powertrain replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned BMW X5 xDrive45e powertrain (B58B30 + e-motor) typically costs £6,500–£10,000 supply only. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,200. Hybrid system work requires specialist training. Battery replacement is a separate cost — approximately £8,000–£12,000.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW X5 xDrive45e reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The xDrive45e combines the proven B58 3.0-litre inline-six with a 105kW electric motor and 24kWh battery. The combustion engine is well-developed and reliable, with cooling system leaks as the main concern. The hybrid system adds complexity, but early owner reports indicate strong reliability. Battery health is the long-term question mark — all lithium-ion batteries degrade over time.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW X5 xDrive45e?

acceptedAnswer.text: PHEV battery degradation (reduced electric range, £8,000–£12,000 replacement), cooling system leaks on the combustion engine (water pump, expansion tank, £400–£1,000), PCV valve/oil separator failure (whistling, oil consumption, £400–£800), and hybrid system software issues (drive mode malfunctions, warning lights, £150–£500 software update).

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW X5 xDrive45e powertrain, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: X5 xDrive45e values range from £30,000–£60,000 depending on year, condition and mileage. A £6,500–£10,000 reconditioned powertrain represents 10–30% of vehicle value — well within our 70% BMW scrap threshold. For most examples, replacement is financially viable. Battery replacement at £8,000–£12,000 is a separate consideration.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW X5 xDrive45e engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The xDrive45e uses the B58B30 engine code — BMW's 3.0-litre inline-six — combined with a 105kW electric motor. This is the same engine as the 40i but with additional hybrid hardware. The total system output is 394bhp. The code is stamped on the engine block; your VIN and vehicle documentation will also confirm the specification.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the battery degradation issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: In many cases, individual battery modules can be replaced (£2,000–£4,000 per module) rather than the entire pack (£8,000–£12,000). A BMW specialist can perform a battery health check to determine which cells have degraded. At the first sign of significant range reduction, book a health check. Regular AC charging (rather than rapid DC charging) and avoiding deep discharges help preserve battery life.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the G05 xDrive45e generation — a single-generation powertrain. Battery replacement is a separate cost — approximately £8,000–£12,000. All pricing is indicative; actual costs vary by mileage, condition and workshop location.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an X5 xDrive45e powertrain typically takes 12–16 hours at £80–£110/hr (independent BMW specialist) or £130–£180/hr (London/SE dealer). Hybrid system work requires specialist training.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW X5 xDrive45e — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Assess battery health; module replacement may be possible
Software update — almost always the fix
Replacement often £6,500–£10,000 — assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This BMW X5 xDrive45e? Check These First
buyIf: * Full BMW service history with documented battery health checks
* Recent software updates applied (PCM, BMS, TCU)
* Low-mileage with documented charging history (AC charging preferred)
* Still under the 6-year/100,000-mile battery warranty (check transferability)
* Cooling system history — water pump and expansion tank checked
avoidIf: * Significant reduction in electric-only range (battery degradation warning)
* Any hybrid system warnings or drive mode malfunctions without a documented fix
* Poor charging history (frequent rapid charging or deep discharges)
* Missing service history — the PHEV system is complex and unforgiving
* Poor service history on the combustion engine — the B58 is robust but needs proper oil

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The xDrive45e is a landmark plug-in hybrid — combining BMW's excellent B58 inline-six with a powerful 105kW electric motor for 394bhp of seamless performance. The combustion engine is the proven B58, while the 24kWh battery offers genuine electric range. Battery health is the long-term question mark — buy on documented history, charge it properly, and this is a genuinely compelling plug-in hybrid SUV.

cta.label: → Compare BMW X5 xDrive45e powertrain prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW X5 xDrive45e (or before you commit to a powertrain replacement), check these:
items: * Battery Health — Check the estimated electric range; any significant drop from the 54-mile (WLTP) claim?
* Charging History — Has the vehicle been charged correctly? Frequent rapid charging can degrade batteries
* Cold Start — Listen for any rattle on startup (B58 should be smooth)
* Service History — Must show hybrid system checks; 8,000–10,000 mile oil changes are essential
* Coolant Level — Check for any loss — water pump and expansion tank are key risks
* PCV System — Any whistling from the engine? Increased oil consumption?
* Hybrid System — Check all drive modes work; any warning lights or messages?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Hybrid system codes are common
